mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which boosts corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series trend upward, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant in multivariate regression over 5-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average during a late-cycle expansion reflects peak manufacturing capacity utilization, which historically precedes short-term equity pullbacks due to overheating concerns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions, or the signal loses predictive power when regime classification is applied.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 230 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >50% of periods with positive PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: High real GDP growth combined with low unemployment creates a synchronized macroeconomic boom that maximizes corporate profit margins, driving strongly positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to yield positive 60-day returns when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% and UNRATE is below 5.0%, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production following a macroeconomic shock indicates rapid supply-side recovery, which restores investor confidence and generates positive short-term equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances following a >1.0% monthly rebound in INDPRO during post-shock recovery phases, or the signal shows no edge in regime-filtered backtests.
